mechanism_narrative: A rising level of total nonfarm payrolls indicates ongoing labor market expansion that boosts household income and aggregate demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following months with positive PAYEMS growth are not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate sustained below 0.25 percent signals an ultra-accommodative monetary stance that suppresses the equity discount rate and incentivizes risk-taking, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered observations during any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly accelerating retail sales and industrial production signal a broad-based demand-supply expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, and their interaction produces stronger positive S&P 500 forward returns than either variable alone over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and INDPRO fails to achieve a t-statistic greater than 2.0 in predicting 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the directional sign flips negative.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated year-over-year inflation rate proxied by a high CPIAUCSL level erodes real corporate margins and prompts tightening expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered periods, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index signals improving manufacturing momentum and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate operating leverage and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.0 index point over prior 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following triggered INDPRO acceleration are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above the 1,000 thousand unit threshold signals a durable goods and construction-led demand impulse that drives cyclical equity outperformance, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1000, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous elevation of unemployment and consumer prices creates a stagflationary interaction that squeezes corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of UNRATE > 6.0 and CPIAUCSL > 230 fails to produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment and retail sales confirms a durable consumption-employment feedback loop that expands equity earnings multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS and RSAFS produces a negative beta on S&P 500 60-day forward returns in any 3-year rolling window, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03.
